sections.paragraph:
-

(1) properly i.q. Arab. حَادَ Med. Ye, to turn aside, II. to tie knots, whence may be derived the Hebr. חִידָה an enigma, a parable, which is joined to this verb, and then it signifies-

(2) to propose an enigma, Jud. 14:12 , seq.; to set forth a parable Eze 17:2. Compare לוּץ and מְלִיצָה and Gr. ἐμπλέκειν αἰνίγματα, Æsch. Prometh. Vinct. 610. (So it is commonly taken, and it is not amiss. It is worthy of examination, however, whether חִידָה may not be used for חִדָּה and signify a smart saying; for חוּד may be a denominative derived from it.) Hence חִידָה, אֲחִידָה.
